title: Manual Dissection of Schistosoma mansoni and S. japonicum for Transcriptomic Analysis
author: Unknown
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.uq3evyn

steps.title: Take the Petri dish out of...
steps.instruction: Take the Petri dish out of the ice bath, touch it against a paper to remove the excess of water, place it under the stereomicroscope and adjust the focus. (Stereo microscope EZ4, Leica, 10447197, Leica EZ4 educational stereomicroscope., https://s3.amazonaws.com/pr-journal/6sb28n6.jpg )

steps.title: Look for a parasite with uncurled head/esophagus
steps.instruction: Look for a parasite with uncurled head/esophagus. Increase the lenses magnification and use your dissecting tools (tweezers and scissors) to keep the head in the field-of-view. (Biology Tweezer 115 mm, Ideal Tak, 7SG.CX.0, Biology Tweezer 115 mm (Ideal Tak Chiasso, Switzerland). Manufacturer Part Number: 7SG.CX.0, https://s3.amazonaws.com/pr-journal/6r928n6.jpg ) (Vannas scissors, John Weiss, 0103123, Vannas scissors (John Weiss, Milton Keynes, UK). Straight with sharp tips., https://s3.amazonaws.com/pr-journal/6sa28n6.jpg )

steps.title: Hold the parasite carefully with the...
steps.instruction: Hold the parasite carefully with the tweezers, positioning it in an angle that allows you to clearly see the suckers ( i.e. lateral view, or suckers facing up), then make the first incision (#1) along the transverse gut ( i.e. where the dark pigmentation of hemozoin starts; indicated by the red arrow in the illustration below).

steps.title: Once the HE fragment is detached it floats around
steps.instruction: Once the HE fragment is detached it floats around. Aspirate it with the RNAlater solution using the P20 micropipette then dispense it inside the HE microtube. Always keep the tube on ice. CRITICAL STEP do not aspirate any other fragment or particulate material with the HE.

steps.title: The final cut (#2) is considerably...
steps.instruction: The final cut (#2) is considerably easier and usually does not require high magnification. Simply cut the posterior third of the parasite body. Collect the BE fragment with the tweezers, transfer it to the BE microtube and keep in the ice box.

steps.title: If needed, you can immerse the...
steps.instruction: If needed, you can immerse the tweezers and scissors in the beaker with deionized water to get rid of salt crystals. Dry your tools in a soft and lint-free paper before continuing the dissection.

steps.title: This will help you to control...
steps.instruction: This will help you to control how long the Petri dish is out of the ice.

steps.title: In Step 2 you started a 10 minutes countdown
steps.instruction: In Step 2 you started a 10 minutes countdown. You can dissect as many worms as you can in this time, but it is important to chill the Petri dish every 10 minutes to preserve your material.  ATTENTION This is an opportunity to transfer the dissected worms to the "dissected body" microtube then add new ones to the Petri dish for the next round. In addition, if you notice that salt crystals are accumulating in the RNAlater solution discard it, wash the Petri dish with deionized water, dry it with lint-free paper and add fresh RNAlater solution. PAUSE POINT If you wish, store the dissected fragments in the fridge and continue the procedure another time.
